title: High Intensity Step Training After Traumatic Brain Injury

skipped_criteria: TBI definition requiring LOC, PTA, or objective neurological findings: no schema field captures clinical TBI diagnostic criteria.
Less than 6 months post injury at time of consent: no schema field for time-since-injury.
Weight less than 298 pounds and height less than 6'2": no schema fields for weight or height.
Intact skin (no open lesions/bandages) in harness contact area: no schema field for skin integrity.
Able to walk ten feet with assistance of not more than two people and/or bracing: no schema field for ambulatory function.
Impaired walking speed of less than 1.4 meters/second: no schema field for gait speed.
Ability to follow directions/standardized instructions: no schema field for cognitive compliance.
Completing or have completed initial inpatient rehabilitation for TBI: no schema field for rehabilitation status.
O-Log score of at least 20: no schema field for O-Log orientation score.
Medically cleared for HIST by a physician: no schema field for physician clearance status.
Uncontrolled cardiopulmonary, metabolic, or infectious disorder: no schema fields for these comorbidities.
History of orthopedic or additional neurological disorder limiting motor function before TBI: no schema field for prior medical history.
Any reason in investigator opinion that would interfere with study protocol (e.g., behavioral concerns): no schema field for investigator judgment.
Uncontrolled seizure disorder: no schema field for seizure history or control status.
Lactating females: no schema field for lactation status.
Current participation in any other interventional trials: no schema field for concurrent trial enrollment.
History of untreated or poorly controlled psychiatric condition (depression, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder): no schema field for psychiatric history.
